rationale: Within a 9-second window, a parent cmd.exe (PID 1208, EV-001/EV-002) spawned two separate cmd.exe child processes, each of which immediately invoked PowerShell to enumerate local user accounts — first via Get-LocalUser (A0, EV-001) and then via Get-WmiObject Win32_UserAccount (EV-002, EV-003). The sequential, scripted pattern of dual local-user enumeration commands issued programmatically through cmd /c is consistent with post-exploitation discovery activity (e.g., an implant or script probing account inventory). However, all processes run at Medium integrity under corp\user-01 with legitimate, signed system binaries, no obfuscation or encoded payloads are present, and the parent cmd.exe (PID 1208) context is not fully explained by the available evidence — a legitimate administrative script or IT tool cannot be ruled out. The absence of any network, file-write, or credential-access events caps confidence that this is malicious rather than authorized reconnaissance or a sysadmin script.
